ui.md_warn: ⚠️ Never enter your seed phrase, private key or wallet password on any site.
ui.ft_links: 📖 What is LGNS? Full intro · 📑 Contracts · ❓ FAQ · 🏛️ About · 🔍 Sources
ui.h_lede: From a single token to a whole blockchain — Web3 explained in plain language. No background needed; walk the Five Trials and you'll get it.
ui.i_sub: Many people are lured by the APY and put money in without knowing what they bought. Here we flip it — first get clear on the three basics.
ui.i_t1:
IWhat is Origin

A whole Web3 ecosystem: at its core is the token LGNS, plus staking, DAO governance and a self-built Anubis privacy chain. Think of it as "an on-chain community with its own token and rules".
ui.i_t2:
IIWhat is LGNS

The ecosystem's core token. Hold and stake it to participate; it uses a game model called (3,3) that rewards long-term holding over short-term trading.
ui.i_t3:
IIIWhat is staking

Locking LGNS into a contract and earning token rewards every 6 hours. Where the yield comes from and whether it's "guaranteed" is broken down in Trials 2–3 — understand it before you act.
ui.p_sub: From “heard of it” to “judge for yourself”. Go in order; the sacred fire lights each rune as you scroll. About 90 minutes total — take it in a few sittings.
ui.s1:
Meet Origin ~10 min

Get the terms straight: what Origin, Anubis and LGNS each are and how they relate.

  • Origin = the whole Web3 ecosystem
  • LGNS = the core token (algorithmic, 9 decimals not 18; governance is a separate token, GLGNS)
  • Anubis = a self-built privacy chain (Chain ID 6714, gas paid in DAI)
ui.s2:
Understand LGNS & (3,3) ~20 min

Grasp what powers this token and why everyone talks about “staking”.

  • (3,3): a game model that rewards everyone holding long-term
  • Staking rewards are essentially protocol emissions every 6 hours (rebase)
  • High APY ≠ net profit — watch both the price and the real reserves
ui.s3:
Try Staking ~20 min

From setting up a wallet to your first stake and claiming rewards — the whole flow.

  • Set up a self-custody wallet and safeguard your seed phrase
  • Learn where to buy LGNS and how to make a transaction
  • Start small, confirm it works, then scale up
ui.s4:
Join Governance ~20 min

Know what Awake DAO is and how a proposal goes from idea to execution.

  • Awake DAO: where the community governs
  • The flow: Proposal → Discussion → Vote → Execution
  • Voting power comes from the GLGNS governance token & governance NFTs (not from holding or staking LGNS directly)
ui.s5:
Advanced & Security ~20 min

Learn to verify data on-chain yourself, spot phishing, and protect yourself.

  • Use a block explorer to check contracts and transactions
  • Beware of new tricks like EIP-7702 approval phishing
  • Stick to official channels and verify on-chain yourself (DYOR)
ui.f_sub: The questions beginners get stuck on. Tap a slab for the answer — we explain “what it is and how to judge”, we don't decide for you.
ui.t_sub: The beauty of Web3 is you can verify everything on-chain yourself. These tools turn “what others say” into “what you checked yourself”.
ui.t_note: ⚠️ Data is read live from the chain and official APIs, for reference only; do real trading / staking through official entrances, always verify contract addresses yourself and beware of phishing.
ui.l_sub: Video courses, PDF slides, articles and posts, all collected here. Filter by category, tap a card to open — videos play, slides flip, articles pop up.
ui.c_sub: Scan to add me — join the community, ask questions, get first-hand info. Don't go it alone as a beginner.
ui.ft_disc: For learning only, not investment advice · DYOR — check it, understand it, then decide.

faqs.q: What's the difference between Origin, Anubis and LGNS?
a: In short: Origin is the whole ecosystem, LGNS is its core token, and Anubis is the blockchain the ecosystem built itself.
Analogy: Origin is a city, LGNS is the currency circulating in it, and Anubis is the ground beneath it (the underlying network).

faqs.q: Where do staking rewards come from? Are they reliable?
a: Staking rewards mainly come from the protocol minting new tokens every 6 hours (rebase / inflationary rewards) — not from thin air.
So the key question isn't the APY, but: do the newly minted tokens have real value behind them? Can the price hold?
Don't judge a project by slogans — check on-chain: where the yield comes from, where funds flow, whether anyone can freely mint or move funds. The On-chain Tools below help you check exactly this.
(3,3) is game-theory shorthand: if everyone chooses to stake and hold long-term, the overall outcome is best for all (hence 3,3).
It uses mechanics to encourage a long-term mindset, but the mechanism itself doesn't guarantee profit — sentiment and real value matter just as much.

faqs.q: Where do I buy LGNS? How do I trade?
a: Usually via decentralized trading tools on the relevant chain; for the exact entrance follow official announcements and don't act through unknown links.
Before your first trade, walk through your wallet and the process, start small, confirm it works, then scale up.

faqs.q: Can I withdraw my staked tokens anytime?
a: Rules differ by staking type — some allow anytime redemption, some have a lock-up. Always read the exact contract rules before you act.
For can-I-get-my-principal-back questions, the most reliable answer is the contract itself, not hearsay.

faqs.q: I heard about EIP-7702 approval phishing — how do I stay safe?
a: EIP-7702 is a newer wallet mechanism that scammers also use to trick you into approving — once you sign a malicious approval, funds can be drained.
Protection: don't click unknown links, don't sign approvals you don't understand, and review your wallet approvals regularly. Treat DMs like free airdrop / recover your stolen coins as scams.

faqs.q: What are the official channels? How do I avoid fakes?
a: Stick to the officially announced accounts (main @SaluteOrigin, chain @ANUBISCHAIN_, etc.), bookmark official links, and never enter through search results or DM links.
Remember one thing: a real official will never DM you for your seed phrase or ask you to transfer coins.
